steps.title: Identify the contrasting eclipse scenarios
steps.content: The passage describes what observers would have witnessed on July 9, 1926 (an annular eclipse) followed by what stargazers would have seen on December 24, 1927 (a partial eclipse). These are two different eclipse events with different visual effects.

steps.title: Apply the signal to the choices
steps.content:
”On the other hand,” introduces a contrasting observation—the transition needed to connect two different eclipse types.

The other choices impose wrong relationships:
  • A wrongly suggests causation between the events
  • C contradicts the different dates by requiring simultaneity
  • D suggests the second observation reinforces rather than contrasts with the first
.

That matches choice B — B is correct.
